description:
**Milestone:** Failure Triage & Constraint Engineering

Add a constraints object to AgentTemplateSchema that allows each template to declaratively flag which safety constraint blocks it needs (worktreeSafety, portProtection, processGuard, monorepoStandards) and any forbidden tool patterns. Update buildSystemPrompt() in dynamic-agent-executor.ts to read these flags and render the corresponding prompt fragments from team-base.ts. Update all built-in templates (pr-maintainer, board-janitor, and any others) to explicitly declare their constraint sets.

Currently WORKTREE_SAFETY, PORT_PROTECTION, PROCESS_GUARD are hardcoded into getEngineeringBase() and composed into agent prompts regardless of template settings. After this change, constraint injection is template-driven and auditable in code. The shared base still includes them by default for engineering agents, but templates can now override or extend.

**Files to Modify:**
- libs/types/src/agent-templates.ts
- apps/server/src/services/dynamic-agent-executor.ts
- apps/server/src/services/built-in-templates.ts

**Acceptance Criteria:**
- [ ] AgentTemplateSchema has a constraints object with worktreeSafety, portProtection, processGuard, monorepoStandards boolean flags and forbiddenPatterns string array
- [ ] buildSystemPrompt() renders constraint blocks from template.constraints flags
- [ ] All built-in templates explicitly declare their constraint sets
- [ ] No existing agent behavior changes (constraints still applied, just now declarative)
- [ ] npm run build:packages passes
- [ ] npm run build:server passes
- [ ] npm run test:server passes

**Complexity:** medium
title: Template-Driven Constraint Declarations
